home.title: Building Communities Where Everyone Belongs Through Sports
home.subtitle: TEAMiN creates inclusive sports environments in Bavaria through our Sport-Assistenz program, connecting people with disabilities to mainstream sports clubs and fostering independence through skilled support.

hero.title: TEAMiN: The Inclusive Network
hero.subtitle: Building communities where everyone belongs through systematic sport assistance and sustainable inclusion.

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.description: TEAMiN is a collaborative network of research institutions, sports organizations, and community partners working together to transform sports culture in Bavaria. We combine academic expertise with practical experience to create lasting change.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We create systematic pathways for inclusion by training qualified sport assistants who enable people with disabilities to participate independently in mainstream sports clubs. Our evidence-based approach ensures sustainable integration.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A Bavaria where every sports club is truly inclusive, where people with disabilities participate as valued members, and where temporary support leads to lasting independence and belonging.

inclusion.heading: Our Understanding of Inclusion
inclusion.description: For us, inclusion means more than just participation – it's about genuine belonging and self-determination. We believe that everyone has the right to experience sports in a supportive environment that addresses individual needs. Through systematic approaches, evidence-based methods, and community-centered support, we create sustainable structures that enable people with disabilities to participate independently in club life over the long term.

team.heading: Our Team
team.subtitle: A diverse group of researchers, practitioners, and community leaders dedicated to making sports accessible for everyone.

members.name: Jakob Prechtl
members.role: Project Member University of Würzburg - Responsible for qualification and research

members.name: Tim Noppeney
members.role: Project Member BVS Bayern - Responsible for user research and marketing

members.name: Michael Zimmermann
members.role: Project Member University of Würzburg - Responsible for qualification and research

partners.heading: Our Partners
partners.bvs: Research partner in the BMFTR funded research project 'SpAss' contributing project members as well as access to e-learning and booking infrastructure and valuable network of sports clubs in Bavaria.
partners.uniWuerzburg: Research partner in the BMFTR funded research project 'SpAss' contributing project members, deep academic knowledge around inclusive sports, qualification for assistants, ongoing research around sports assistance.
partners.bmftr: Funds the research project via the datipilot program.
partners.lebenshilfe: They match athletes and volunteers and take care of all administration.
partners.adidas: Supports the project as a strategic partner through employee volunteering in the form of pro bono consulting and sports assistants as well as financial support.

spass.title: Sport-Assistenz (SpAss) Program
spass.subtitle: Our structured training program creates skilled assistants who provide temporary support, enabling independent participation in mainstream sports clubs.

features.title: Structured Training
features.description: Comprehensive methodology based on research and best practices for inclusive sports assistance.

features.title: Temporary Support
features.description: Skilled assistance that gradually reduces as independence and club integration develops.

features.title: Sustainable Inclusion
features.description: Long-term integration into mainstream clubs with ongoing community support systems.

athletes.title: For Athletes & Families
athletes.description: We connect people with disabilities and their families to sports opportunities throughout Bavaria, providing the support needed to find your place in mainstream sports clubs.

aurelian.title: Meet Aurelian

hero.quote: Through TEAMiN's Sport-Assistenz program, I found not just a sport I love, but a community that embraces me for who I am. The support was there when I needed it, and now I'm proud to call my teammates true friends.
hero.description: Aurelian's story shows how TEAMiN's Sport-Assistenz program creates real opportunities for athletes with disabilities. Through qualified support and systematic integration, he found not just a sport he loves, but a community where he truly belongs. Today, Aurelian plays independently with his team, having developed the skills and confidence to participate fully in mainstream sports.

aurelian.quoteShort: Through TEAMiN's program, I found not just a sport, but a community that accepts me. Now I'm proud to call my teammates true friends.
aurelian.descriptionShort: Through qualified support and systematic integration, Aurelian found a community where he truly belongs. Today, he plays independently with his team.
aurelian.quote: Through TEAMiN's Sport-Assistenz program, I found not just a sport I love, but a community that embraces me for who I am. The support was there when I needed it, and now I'm proud to call my teammates true friends.

cookieConsent.title: Cookie Preferences
cookieConsent.subtitle: We respect your privacy
cookieConsent.description: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience, analyze site usage, and assist with our marketing efforts. This helps us provide you with better content and a more personalized experience.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: These cookies are necessary for the website to function properly. They enable basic features like page navigation, language preferences, and access to secure areas.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: We use Microsoft Clarity and Google Analytics to understand how visitors interact with our website. This helps us improve our content and user experience while maintaining your privacy.

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.description: Just like in sports, sometimes we take a wrong turn. But don't worry - this isn't a barrier we can't overcome. Let's get you back on track and find the path to what you're looking for.

hero.title: Sport. Together. Inclusive.
hero.subtitle: Belonging and moving together – it doesn't get more human than that. For many, these are still dreams because barriers hold them back. We're changing that: together with you, we create paths to teams that last.

whyMatters.description: Sports should be a place where everyone can participate, grow, and belong. Yet many people with disabilities face barriers that prevent them from joining mainstream sports clubs. You have the power to bridge this gap and create meaningful change in your community through our Sport-Assistenz program.

becomeAssistant.title: For Sport Assistants
becomeAssistant.description: Accompany others on their sports journey and become part of the community

athletesFamilies.title: For Athletes
athletesFamilies.description: Find your place on the team with qualified support

spAssProgram.title: SpAss Program
spAssProgram.description: Explore our sport assistance program in detail

teamInNetwork.title: TEAMiN - The Inclusive Community
teamInNetwork.description: Discover the people and organizations behind the community

hero.title: Are you the bridge?
hero.subtitle: Someone is waiting to finally just be part of a team - the way many others already are. You can be the difference. We'll help you really make an impact with your personality and skills.

benefits.title: Why become a Sport Assistant?
benefits.subtitle: Share what you have - and grow beyond yourself.

cards.title: Share what matters to you
cards.description: Experience, energy, empathy - you have so much to give and you know yourself how important it is to be part of a team. Here you can share exactly that with someone.

cards.title: Stay flexible and individual
cards.description: You as an assistance match decide: when, how often, which sport. Together you'll find a way that fits your schedules, skills and needs.

cards.title: Use and develop your skills
cards.description: Empathy. Creativity. Openness. Courage. As sport assistance, you use what's already in you - and you develop further.

cards.title: Just be human
cards.description: No performance, no script. It's about being authentic, listening, being there. What's human counts - and that's exactly how you make the difference.

testimonials.quote: I think it's very fulfilling work. You experience so much from the lives of those you accompany. You can celebrate successes and see directly what impact you have.
testimonials.name: Kristina, Scout Group Leader

testimonials.quote: Just seeing her joy - how proud and happy she was on that stage, how the children celebrated together over that performance. That was just mega cool.
testimonials.name: Emma, Dance Assistant

testimonials.quote: Once a month we go to the Sunday disco together. It's hard for me to stop because we've built a connection. She wouldn't have anyone else - and I personally enjoy it too.
testimonials.name: Mina, Special Education Teacher & Leisure Assistant

journey.title: Your Journey as a Sport Assistant
journey.subtitle: Four steps to real impact

steps.title: Get Qualified
steps.description: E-Learning online + practice day on-site. Free and flexible. You learn everything you need - without application stress.

steps.title: Get Matched
steps.description: Our partners like Lebenshilfe connect you with athletes who match your skills and schedule.

steps.title: Accompany and Support
steps.description: From getting to know each other to finding sports to club integration: You're there - with transportation, networking, encouragement. Exactly as needed.

steps.title: Foster Independence
steps.description: Your goal: That your support eventually becomes unnecessary. Because relationships in the team have grown. Because self-confidence is there. Because inclusion works.

options.title: Sign Up
options.description: Start now with the free online qualification and practice day. No application, just get started.

options.title: Meet Aurelian
options.description: Talk to our project leader about the program, your questions or your individual situation.

options.title: Contact
options.description: Write us your questions, ideas or concerns. We'll get back to you quickly.

hero.title: Ready to make a difference?
hero.subtitle: Join our community of sport assistants. Get certified with flexible online learning and hands-on practice.

practiceDay.description: Practice real-word scenarios in our hands-on training session with your fellow aspiring assistants.

partner.title: Partners
partner.description: Your employer is already a partner of ours? Find out how to join.

training.note: The E-Learning and our Practice Day booking are hosted by our partner Behinderten- und Rehabilitationssportverband Bayern e.V.

testimonials.quote: Through TEAMiN's Sport-Assistenz program, I found not just a sport I love, but a community that embraces me. The support was there when I needed it, and now I'm proud to call my teammates true friends.
testimonials.name: Aurelian, Football Player

testimonials.quote: Being a Sport Assistant has been incredibly rewarding. Watching athletes gain confidence and independence while building lasting friendships makes every moment worthwhile.
testimonials.name: Sarah M., Sport Assistant

testimonials.quote: Our club has been transformed by the SpAss program. We've welcomed new members, learned valuable skills, and created a truly inclusive environment.

assistant.title: Assistant Pathway
assistant.description: Use your passion for sports to make a meaningful impact. Get certified training and support athletes on their journey to independence.

athlete.title: Athlete Pathway
athlete.description: Find your place in sports with qualified support. Join a mainstream club with assistance tailored to help you succeed and belong.

organization.title: For Organizations
organization.description: Partner with TEAMiN to build inclusive sports programs. Access training, resources, and support to transform your club culture.

items.question: Who can participate in the SpAss program?
items.answer: The program is open to people with disabilities of all ages who want to participate in mainstream sports clubs with qualified support.

items.question: How long does the support last?
items.answer: Support duration varies based on individual needs. The goal is to gradually reduce assistance as you become integrated and independent in your club.

items.question: What sports are available?
items.answer: We work with a wide range of sports clubs across Bavaria. We'll help match you with a sport and club that fits your interests and abilities.

items.question: What training is required?
items.answer: All assistants complete our comprehensive Sport-Assistenz certification program, which includes online modules, practical training, and ongoing support.

items.question: How much time commitment is needed?
items.answer: Time commitment varies based on your availability and the athlete's schedule. Most assistants commit to 2-4 hours per week.

items.question: Do I need sports expertise?
items.answer: While sports interest is important, you don't need to be an expert. We provide training in both sports assistance and inclusive support methods.

items: Obtain information about your data stored with us
Request correction of inaccurate personal data
Request deletion of your data stored with us
Object to the processing of your data

yourRole.title: Your Role as an Assistant
yourRole.subtitle: You'll learn to:
points: Connect athletes with the right sports clubs and opportunities
Coordinate between athletes, families, clubs, and trainers
Provide hands-on support during training and competitions
Enable communication and self-determination for athletes

hero.title: INCLUSIVE TEAMS THROUGH SPORT ASSISTANCE
hero.subtitle: We leverage the power of communities to support athletes' independent participation in regular sports clubs.

origins.title: THE ORIGINS OF SpAss
origins.subtitle: From sport assistance research to network
origins.description1: It will run until March 2026 and is headed by the Universität Würzburg-based researcher Dr. Christiane Reuter and BVS Bayern Vice President Prof. Dr. David Rygl.
origins.description2: Based on a pilot in Würzburg by Dr. Reuter in 2023, the ongoing research project focuses on how to integrate our sport assistance solution into existing communities, organizations, and institutions through diverse partnerships.
origins.description3: To this end, we use design-based research and state-of-the-art marketing to create attractive, user-centered solutions. Our dual focus—research and design—generates valuable data on inclusive sports participation while building sustainable pathways towards inclusive sports communities.

together.title: HAVING SpAss TOGETHER
together.description: Our sportassistant solution SpAss is made possible by many diverse partners. From the funding we receive from the BMFTR to the deep knowledge and networks shared by BVS Bayern and Uni Würzburg to the strategic support from adidas corporate volunteers.

steps.title: Assistant Qualification
steps.description: Aspiring assistants complete comprehensive training: 15 hours of self-paced e-learning and a full-day hands-on workshop.

steps.title: Registration & Matching
steps.description: Athletes and assistants register with partnering organizations like Lebenshilfe. Based on preferences and availability, matching happens through the ava platform.

steps.title: First Steps & Sports Participation
steps.description: Athletes and assistants get to know each other, choose the sport and club together. The supportive community is there for the first sessions and beyond.

steps.title: Fostering Independence
steps.description: The goal is always independence through personal growth, strong team relationships, and becoming a fully part of their teams.

qualification.title: State-of-the-Art Sport Assistant Qualification
qualification.subtitle: Making SpAss safe and effective through online and in-person training

items.question: What is TEAMiN?
items.answer: TEAMiN is a network for promoting inclusive sports in Bavaria through the Sport-Assistenz (SpAss) program. We connect people with disabilities with qualified trained sport assistants and sports clubs.

items.question: What is the SpAss Program?
items.answer: SpAss (Sport-Assistenz) is our comprehensive training program that prepares volunteers to support athletes with disabilities in mainstream sports clubs. The program includes online learning and practical training.

items.question: How can I participate?
items.answer: You can either participate as a sport assistant (after completing the training) or use our placement services as an athlete with disabilities. Contact us for more information.

items.question: What are the requirements?
items.answer: You need an interest in sports, empathy, and the willingness to support people with disabilities. Sports experience is helpful but not required. The training is free of charge.

items.question: How much time commitment is required?
items.answer: Initial training: ~15 hours online + 1 full practice day. Ongoing commitment: 2-4 hours per week for at least 3 months. The schedule is flexible and coordinated with your matched athlete.

items.question: Will I be compensated?
items.answer: The work as a sport assistant is voluntary. However, you receive free, certified training and valuable experience in inclusive sports.

items.question: What happens after the training?
items.answer: After successful completion, you will be matched with athletes and clubs that fit your skills and schedule. You continue to receive support through our network.

items.question: Who can use the service?
items.answer: People with disabilities of all ages who want to participate in club sports and need support.

items.question: Which sports are available?
items.answer: We work with various sports clubs across Bavaria offering different sports. We help match you with a sport and club that fits your interests and abilities.

items.question: How long does the support last?
items.answer: The duration varies depending on individual needs. The goal is to gradually reduce support as you integrate into the club and become more independent.

items.question: What does participation cost?
items.answer: The placement and support through TEAMiN is free. Regular club membership fees apply for club membership.

items.question: How can our club become a partner?
items.answer: Contact us directly. We support clubs in becoming more inclusive through consultation, trained sport assistants, and best practice exchange.

items.question: What support do clubs receive?
items.answer: Partner clubs get access to trained sport assistants, guidance on inclusive practices, networking opportunities, and support in integrating athletes with disabilities.

moreQuestions.title: More Questions?
moreQuestions.description: If you have additional questions, feel free to contact us!
